title: Per-transport heap bundles ctx+cfg+vtable+CString in one Box allocation
description: TcpInner/UdsInner/ShmInner each pack the C context struct, config struct, vtable, and the CString (IP/path/name) into one Box. This is correct and important: the C config struct holds a raw pointer (server_ip_string, server_path, name) into the CString data. By placing both in the same struct on the heap, the pointer and its target share a single allocation lifetime — they cannot be separated. A future contributor might suggest breaking these apart (e.g. keeping CStrings in a Vec<CString> or in the Client struct directly) to 'simplify', but that would introduce a gap where the config struct exists with a dangling pointer between allocation and connection. The single-bundle approach eliminates this class of bug structurally.
